what_it_does: Defines window.CV_SHOT, an IIFE that gives AtomiCity/Vi visual capture and a rolling image memory. It lazily loads the snapdom library from a CDN, captures a dataURL PNG of a selected element (snapshot) or the whole #atomicity-root/body (snapshotPage), keeps a FIFO-capped history of captured images (default limit 8, adjustable 1-40 by user or AI), and produces before→after pairs around an action (aroundChange) for visual diffing. On load it also registers two capabilities into CV_AI: shot.setLimit (AI tunes its own visual memory) and vision.diff (compare before/after with a vision provider, throws in sandbox).
observations: Capture uses snapdom.toCanvas (scale capped at 2 / devicePixelRatio, transparent background) and returns a PNG dataURL
ensureLib lazily injects a script tag and fails soft (resolves null on error) so callers fall back to the computed-style text diff
remember() pushes {id,url,meta,at} and shifts oldest when over limit (FIFO)
aroundChange captures before, runs the action (catching errors into result), waits one rAF + 60ms for paint to settle, captures after, remembers the after-shot
Both CV_AI registrations are wrapped in try/catch and use {silent:true}
vision.diff throws explicitly when no vision runtime exists, with a message telling the user to export with a vision model
setLimit clamps to 1-40 and trims history immediately
standouts: Before→after visual pairing so a change can be judged by sight, with a deliberate paint-settle delay before the after-shot
The AI can adjust its own visual memory size via a registered capability (shot.setLimit)
snapdom is chosen specifically because it understands modern CSS color (oklch/color-mix) unlike html2canvas
conventions: Registers its capabilities INTO CV_AI rather than maintaining a parallel UI list (interface = projection of registry)
Fail-soft on the optional capture lib (returns null) but loud-throw inside vision.diff when the runtime is genuinely absent
IIFE + window global + subscribe pattern shared across atomicity layer
voice.conceptv sourced as a behaviour reference on vision.diff rather than inlined
concerns: Header docstring says capture uses html2canvas via pinned CDN, but the code actually loads and uses snapdom — the comment is stale relative to the implementation
Loads a third-party library from an external unpkg CDN at runtime (network dependency / supply-chain surface)
emit() swallows listener errors with an empty catch
snapshotPage targets a hardcoded #atomicity-root selector with a body fallback
notes: The vision/memory layer: snapshot any element or the page to a PNG dataURL, keep a small FIFO image history, and capture before/after pairs so Vi can literally see whether a change worked — falling back to text diff when capture or a vision runtime is unavailable.
questions: Was the html2canvas reference in the header intended to be updated to snapdom, or is one of the two the intended library?
Which callers consume aroundChange and the history (the chat UI, the inspector)?
What is the 'vision' provider's runtime and when is it present outside the sandbox?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly match — the implemented behaviour (capture, FIFO memory, before/after pairs, CV_AI capability registration) matches the stated purpose; the only divergence is the header naming html2canvas while the code uses snapdom.
apparent_themes: Letting the AI see (vision as first-class)
Fail-soft optional capability, loud-fail genuine absence
Capabilities registered into the AI registry, not a parallel surface
intention_signals: Tries to make visual verification real — give Vi eyes so it can snapshot a change, compare before/after, and decide whether the intent was achieved — while degrading gracefully to text diffs when capture or a vision model is unavailable.
novelty: The combination of element-scoped snapshotting, a self-tunable FIFO image memory, and an AI-registered before/after vision-diff capability (with snapdom's modern-color support) is the distinctive element.
contribution: Contributes the visual capture and image-memory substrate: element/page snapshots to PNG dataURLs, a FIFO rolling history the chat and vision provider consume, and before→after pairing plus a registered vision-diff capability that lets Vi judge a change by sight (with text-diff fallback).
summary_for_embedding: A JS IIFE (window.CV_SHOT) that snapshots elements/pages to PNG dataURLs via snapdom, keeps a FIFO-capped image history, and produces before→after pairs, registering CV_AI capabilities for self-tuning memory and vision-based change diffing.
intention_for_embedding: To give Vi visual perception and memory so it can snapshot and visually verify before/after of a UI change, degrading to a text diff when capture or vision is unavailable.
